response:
No two adjacent words can start with consecutive letters of the alphabet.

To find the speed of the sailboat when wind power is maximum, we first express power \( N \) as the product of force \( F \) and boat speed \( v \):

\[ N = F \cdot v = \frac{B S \rho (v_0 - v)^2}{2} \cdot v \]

Since \( B \), \( S \), \( \rho \), and \( v_0 \) are constants, maximizing \( N \) is equivalent to maximizing the function \( f(v) = v(v_0 - v)^2 \).

We take the derivative of \( f(v) \) with respect to \( v \) and set it to zero:

\[ \frac{d}{dv} [v(v_0 - v)^2] = (v_0 - v)^2 + v \cdot 2(v_0 - v)(-1) = 0 \]

Factor out \( (v_0 - v) \):

\[ (v_0 - v) [ (v_0 - v) - 2v ] = 0 \]

This gives two solutions:
1.  \( v_0 - v = 0 \implies v = v_0 \) (This yields zero power, so it is a minimum).
2.  \( v_0 - v - 2v = 0 \implies v_0 = 3v \implies v = \frac{v_0}{3} \) (This yields the maximum power).

Substitute the given wind speed \( v_0 = 6.3 \, \text{m/s} \):

\[ v = \frac{6.3}{3} = 2.1 \, \text{m/s} \]

The speed of the sailboat at this moment is 2.1 m/s.
